task.title: MDITA Task Validation Test
task.shortdesc: Exercises all elements permitted in the MDITA task shell: prereq, context, steps with substeps and choices, steps-unordered, result, tasktroubleshooting, postreq, notes, and profiling.

prereq.p: Before you begin, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

ul.li: The oc CLI installed and configured.
Administrator access to the openshift-config namespace.

prereq.note: Back up your kubeconfig before proceeding.

context.p: This procedure installs and configures the monitoring stack on . The monitoring stack collects metrics from cluster components and user workloads.
For details, see Monitoring Guide or the upstream documentation.
context.codeblock: apiVersion: v1 kind: ConfigMap metadata: name: cluster-monitoring-config namespace: openshift-monitoring data: config.yaml: | enableUserWorkload: true

section.title: AWS-specific considerations
section.p: On AWS, ensure that the ebs-csi-driver is installed for persistent storage.

step.cmd: Log in to the cluster as an administrator.

info.note: Use oc whoami to verify your identity.

info.p: The configuration file controls which components are enabled.

substep.note: Do not modify the cluster-monitoring-config ConfigMap while Prometheus is restarting.
substep.cmd: Create the ConfigMap in the openshift-monitoring namespace.

info.codeblock: oc get configmap cluster-monitoring-config -n openshift-monitoring -o yaml

substep.cmd: Wait for the Prometheus pods to restart.

step.cmd: Choose a storage backend for metrics retention.

choices.choice: Use ephemeral storage (default, no persistence across restarts).
Use a PersistentVolumeClaim for durable storage.
Use an external Thanos object store.

step.note: This step modifies cluster-wide alerting rules. Coordinate with your operations team.

info.codeblock: apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1 kind: PrometheusRule metadata: name: custom-alerts namespace: openshift-user-workload-monitoring spec: groups: - name: pod-alerts rules: - alert: PodCrashLooping expr: rate(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[15m]) > 0 for: 1h

step.cmd: Open the Monitoring page in the OpenShift Web Console and navigate to ObserveMetrics.
For Azure clusters, configure the managed identity for Thanos access.

result.p: The monitoring stack is installed and collecting metrics.
All pods should show Running status.
result.note: It may take up to 5 minutes for all pods to reach a running state.

tasktroubleshooting.p: If monitoring pods are not starting:

ol.li: Check events: oc get events -n openshift-monitoring --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'
Check pod logs: oc logs -n openshift-monitoring prometheus-k8s-0
Verify the ConfigMap: oc get cm cluster-monitoring-config -n openshift-monitoring -o yaml

tasktroubleshooting.note: Error OOMKilled indicates insufficient memory. Increase the node memory or reduce retention period.

postreq.p: After the monitoring stack is running:

ul.li: Set up Grafana dashboards for visualization.
